task_title: Add X social signals collection to intelligence-collector
method: Track A is an estimated historical baseline from similar manual multi-agent tasks; Track B is the observed agent-relay-assisted Codex/Claude workflow for the X social signals task.
confidence: medium-low for time metrics, medium for coordination metrics, high for review/test outcomes

issues_caught_by_codex_review: Default X API call could spend credits unintentionally without an explicit enable switch
Dedup restoration could restore social/GitHub items into news recovery flow
X max_results lacked the documented 10-item lower bound
X endpoint still used api.twitter.com instead of api.x.com
Initial MCP query was too broad and collected crypto/noise
Default social signal scope was too narrow and did not match the user's three-layer target
final_scope: Priority 1: AI industry chain, AI coding, AI researchers/practitioners
Priority 2: developer tool product opportunities, indie hacker, AI SaaS

interpretation_notes: The strongest measured value was review quality: Codex review found concrete safety, billing, and filtering issues before daily automation was enabled.
The strongest workflow value was traceability: each phase had a named status, test count, and explicit next action.
The weakest point remains coordination friction: the user still had to manually switch writer/reviewer roles and relay instructions to Claude.
Time-to-done is intentionally omitted because no precise start/end timestamps were captured.
This trial supports continuing agent-relay, but the next product improvement should target automatic writer lease/handoff and trial data capture rather than more feature expansion.
